provenance.source_corpus: Hydrological changes following restoration of the Bois-des-Bel Peatland, Quebec, 1999–2002

source_spans.text: Restoration measures were undertaken in the fall of 1999 on fields 1–8
straw mulch was spread (4000 kg/ha)
Old drainage ditches were blocked with well-decomposed peat
The construction of bunds have also contributed to increased retention of snowmelt water
Evaporation ( Table 1 ) was less at the restored site
Site runoff decreased in the years following restoration
Higher seasonal mean water table... were observed at the restored site
Higher seasonal mean water table and soil moisture were observed at the restored site
Mean soil–water pressure was also higher than at the unrestored site
indicating rewetting of the peat profile
Seasonal soil volume change (swelling and shrinking of the peat) was evident
Seasonal changes in peat volume also influenced Kh at the restored site, decreasing during low wt periods

nodes.name: hydrological conditions necessary for Sphagnum recolonization

source_spans.text: The restoration techniques contributed to the recovery of hydrological conditions necessary for Sphagnum recolonization

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:e8e2bb23dfa6
edges.original_sentence: Restoration measures were undertaken in the fall of 1999 on fields 1–8 (restored site, 8.1 ha) while field 9 was left as a buffer and fields 10–11 remained in their abandoned state for comparison purposes (unrestored site, 1.9 ha).

source_spans.text: Restoration measures were undertaken in the fall of 1999 on fields 1–8 (restored site, 8.1 ha) while field 9 was left as a buffer and fields 10–11 remained in their abandoned state for comparison purposes (unrestored site, 1.9 ha).

source_document.title: Hydrological changes following restoration of the Bois-des-Bel Peatland, Quebec, 1999–2002

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:4cdac4e40cf4
edges.original_sentence: The addition of the straw mulch contributed to a 13% and 8% decrease in evaporation relative to an adjacent unrestored section in 2000 and 2001, respectively

source_spans.text: The addition of the straw mulch contributed to a 13% and 8% decrease in evaporation relative to an adjacent unrestored section in 2000 and 2001, respectively

source_document.title: Hydrological changes following restoration of the Bois-des-Bel Peatland, Quebec, 1999–2002

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:e8e2bb23dfa6
edges.original_sentence: Site runoff decreased in the years following restoration, both in terms of absolute magnitude and also relative to measured rainfall.

source_spans.text: Site runoff decreased in the years following restoration, both in terms of absolute magnitude and also relative to measured rainfall.

source_document.title: Hydrological changes following restoration of the Bois-des-Bel Peatland, Quebec, 1999–2002

edges.original_sentence: Higher seasonal mean water table and soil moisture were observed at the restored site relative to the unrestored site in 2000 through 2002
